author: Spärck Jones (1972)
title: A Statistical Interpretation of Term Specificity and Its Application in Retrieval
why: Asal-usul IDF: kata langka lebih penting. Dasar TF-IDF, gampang dicerna.

author: Salton & Buckley (1988)
title: Term-weighting approaches in automatic text retrieval
why: Resep TF-IDF klasik buat bobot kata di IR, bacaan wajib pemula.

author: Mikolov et al. (2013)
title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
why: Lahirnya word2vec (CBOW & skip-gram), embedding kata yang legendaris.

author: Mikolov et al. (2013)
title: Distributed Representations of Words and Phrases and their Compositionality
why: Trik negative sampling bikin word2vec cepat; king-man+woman=queen.

author: Pennington et al. (2014)
title: GloVe: Global Vectors for Word Representation
why: Embedding dari matriks co-occurrence global, saingan word2vec.

author: Joulin et al. (2016)
title: Bag of Tricks for Efficient Text Classification
why: fastText: klasifikasi teks super cepat, baseline sederhana yang kuat.

author: Bojanowski et al. (2017)
title: Enriching Word Vectors with Subword Information
why: fastText subword: embedding paham n-gram huruf, tahan kata OOV.

author: Kim (2014)
title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Sentence Classification
why: CNN buat teks: simpel, cepat, hasil klasifikasi kalimat oke banget.

author: Le & Mikolov (2014)
title: Distributed Representations of Sentences and Documents
why: doc2vec: satu vektor buat kalimat/dokumen, lanjutan natural word2vec.

author: Zhang et al. (2015)
title: Character-level Convolutional Networks for Text Classification
why: CNN level karakter, teks mentah tanpa kata pun bisa diklasifikasi.

author: Brown et al. (1992)
title: Class-Based n-gram Models of Natural Language
why: n-gram berbasis kelas kata + Brown clustering, cikal-bakal embedding.

author: Chen & Goodman (1996)
title: An Empirical Study of Smoothing Techniques for Language Modeling
why: Panduan smoothing n-gram; kenapa perlu Kneser-Ney dijelasin jelas.

author: Ratnaparkhi (1996)
title: A Maximum Entropy Model for Part-Of-Speech Tagging
why: POS tagging klasik pakai maxent, pintu masuk sequence labeling.

author: Toutanova et al. (2003)
title: Feature-Rich Part-of-Speech Tagging with a Cyclic Dependency Network
why: Stanford tagger; fitur kaya bikin akurasi POS nyaris 97%.

author: Bengio et al. (2003)
title: A Neural Probabilistic Language Model
why: LM neural pertama; ganti n-gram tabel jadi embedding + jaringan.

author: Mikolov et al. (2010)
title: Recurrent neural network based language model
why: RNN buat language model, konteks panjang tanpa batas jendela n-gram.

author: Hochreiter & Schmidhuber (1997)
title: Long Short-Term Memory
why: LSTM: gerbang lupa/ingat atasi vanishing gradient, fondasi RNN modern.

author: Cho et al. (2014)
title: Learning Phrase Representations using RNN Encoder-Decoder for Statistical Machine Translation
why: Perkenalkan GRU + arsitektur encoder-decoder buat terjemahan.

author: Sutskever et al. (2014)
title: Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks
why: seq2seq murni LSTM; template dasar semua model generatif teks.

author: Bahdanau et al. (2014)
title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
why: Attention lahir di sini; decoder bisa lirik kata sumber relevan.

author: Luong et al. (2015)
title: Effective Approaches to Attention-based Neural Machine Translation
why: Attention global vs lokal, versi rapi & praktis dari Bahdanau.

author: Huang et al. (2015)
title: Bidirectional LSTM-CRF Models for Sequence Tagging
why: BiLSTM-CRF, resep standar POS/NER yang gampang direplikasi.

author: Lample et al. (2016)
title: Neural Architectures for Named Entity Recognition
why: NER neural tanpa fitur manual, char+word BiLSTM-CRF jadi rujukan.

author: Ma & Hovy (2016)
title: End-to-end Sequence Labeling via Bi-directional LSTM-CNNs-CRF
why: BiLSTM-CNN-CRF end-to-end, sequence labeling tanpa preprocessing ribet.

author: Collobert et al. (2011)
title: Natural Language Processing (Almost) from Scratch
why: Satu jaringan buat banyak tugas NLP, awal era neural NLP serba-guna.

author: Blei et al. (2003)
title: Latent Dirichlet Allocation
why: LDA topic model; temukan tema tersembunyi dokumen, matematikanya nendang.
